titleStmt.title: SII 35.198: original edition by D. M. Nagaraju
No. 198. (A.R. No. 87 of 1952-53.) KŌḶIVĀḌ, HUBLI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

summary.p: Pillar standing in front of the Nārāyaṇa temple.

This interesting record is dated Śaka 1485, Rudhirōdgārin, Pushya śu. 15, Wednesday, lunar eclipse. (1563 A.D. December 29) States that Sūrappayya, son of Āneya Dēvappayya, of Agastyagōtra, Āśvalāyana-sūtra and Ṛik-śākhā, who was chief (yajamāna) of the agrahāra of Kōḷivāḍa, presented a woman named Sūriyamāṇiki as kāṇāchiya-pātra (a hereditary dancing girl) to the temple of Mādhava-Janārdana for services therein. He also made provision for her livelihood by means of her receiving from different lands fixed amount of corn and money income of six ghaṭṭi-varāhas per annum.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śubhamastu svasti śrījayābhyudaya śālivā
hanaśakavaruṣa 1485 neya rudhirōdgārisaṁvatsarada
puṣya śudha 15 budhavāradalu śrīmatu Agastyagōtrada Ā
śvalāyanasūtrada rukkuśākheya kōḍivāḍada Agrahā
rada yajamāna Āneyadēvappayyagaḷa maga sūrappayyagaḷu
śrī mādhavajanā¨rdhanasvāmiya guḍiya pātrakke sōmō
parāgapuṇyakāladalu tiramalabōvana magaḷu sūriya
māṇiki yaṁba heṁṇanu kāṇāciya paravāgi sama¨rpaṇe
ya māḍidanu Ā heṁṇige māḍisida svāsthejīvita varuṣa 1 kke
ghaṭṭivaraha Āṟu naṁnā kṣētragaḷa hasigeyali mēṭige
voṁmanada kaṭṭaḷeyallū dhāṁnyavanu biṭṭukoṭṭanu
Eṁdu baresida śilāśāsana [.]
Digital edition of SII 35.198 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
